title: Third Party Installment Sale for Gain Deferral

requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must structure sale of appreciated property using installment method receiving payments over multiple years to defer gain recognition, manage tax brackets, and potentially reduce overall tax liability through strategic timing
criteria: Sell property and receive at least one payment in tax year after year of sale to qualify for installment method
Installment method available for capital gains on sale of real estate, business assets, and other property
Gain recognized proportionally as payments received based on gross profit percentage
Must elect out of installment treatment if prefer immediate gain recognition (e.g., to use expiring losses)
Interest must be charged on deferred payments at applicable federal rate (AFR) or higher
Installment method not available for inventory, publicly traded securities, or depreciation recapture (recapture recognized in year of sale)
Use installment sale to spread gain over multiple years managing marginal tax brackets and NIIT thresholds
Document sale terms, payment schedule, interest rate, and security interest in contract

installment_sale_rules.gain_deferral: Recognize gain proportionally as payments received; defers tax and cash receipt aligned
installment_sale_rules.gross_profit_percentage: Gain calculation: (Selling Price - Basis) ÷ Contract Price; applied to each payment
installment_sale_rules.interest_income: Interest on installment payments taxed as ordinary income when received
installment_sale_rules.bracket_management: Spread gain over multiple years can keep taxpayer in lower brackets and reduce NIIT
installment_sale_rules.third_party_sale: Seller receives installment payments from buyer; not related to §1031 exchange
installment_sale_rules.security_interest: Promissory note and mortgage/security agreement document payment obligation

restrictions: Depreciation recapture (§1245, §1250) recognized in full in year of sale; cannot be deferred
Inventory and dealer property sales not eligible for installment method treatment
Related party sales subject to special look-through rules if related party resells property within 2 years
Pledging installment obligation as security for loan may trigger gain recognition as payment received
Sale or transfer of installment obligation generally triggers recognition of remaining deferred gain
State tax treatment varies; some states don't recognize federal installment method requiring full gain recognition
Imputed interest rules apply if stated interest below AFR; may recharacterize principal as interest
Default risk: seller bears credit risk of buyer over installment period; should evaluate creditworthiness
